title: Mattel RC babies — Jennie Gymnast, Baby Rollerblade, and Baby Walk 'n' Roll
description: Three separate early-1990s Mattel RC babies in one workshop batch: Jennie the gymnast (handset and switch path), the roller-skate baby (stripped gear and leg alignment), and the baby walker (gearbox, contacts, board, and signal path) — each needed deep electronics and mechanical work.
itemDetails: Workshop photos show three separate retail SKUs from Mattel's early-1990s battery remote-control baby doll segment — each sold on its own card or box, not a factory three-in-one set. They are not Playmates Amazing Babies (that line used sound/IR “smart” babies in the ~2000 era, a different mechanism and brand). The set lines up with owner identification as Jennie Gymnast (gymnastic Jenny) on the wired-handset gymnast doll, Baby Rollerblade on the pink inline-skate toddler, and Baby Walk 'n' Roll on the peach-and-teal motorized walker with joystick remote — all Mattel, early 1990s RC format. Typical build is vinyl toddler body, rooted blonde hair, molded diaper or fabric leotard, and C-cell gearmotor drives with wired remotes. Collector interest is vintage-Mattel niche; value rises with working remotes, intact skates/walker, and clean battery trays.
repairDetails: Jennie Gymnast (gymnastic Jenny): we replaced the controller switch, removed a faulty connector and damaged cable section, and brought the handset path back to reliable continuity. Baby Rollerblade (roller-skate baby): a broken gear in the drive was replaced, leg position was readjusted so the skating motion tracks straight, and the mechanism was re-timed after reassembly. Baby Walk 'n' Roll: this one was the longest troubleshoot — a broken gear inside the walker gearbox, tired battery contacts, circuit board faults, and a damaged signal track between board and motors. We replaced the gear, renewed contacts, repaired the board, rebuilt the signal path, and lubricated the drivetrain so the walker responds smoothly to the joystick again.
